summary.intent: Implement a Nostr DM channel for the Moltis messaging platform, bringing it to parity with existing channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, MS Teams, WhatsApp, Matrix) with full NIP-04/NIP-44 support, OTP access control, relay health tracking, web UI, and documentation.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the `moltis-nostr` crate (~1700 LOC) with plugin lifecycle, config, relay subscription bus, outbound DM, access control, seen-event dedup, NIP-01 profile publishing, metrics instrumentation, NIP-44 fallback decryption, and integration tests. Web UI add/edit modals, onboarding flow, E2E tests, documentation, and website icon were also completed. All review comments were resolved across multiple Greptile and human review rounds.

repo: Channel plugins follow a strict pattern: ChannelType variant → crate with plugin.rs/config.rs/bus.rs/outbound.rs → feature-flag chain cli→httpd→gateway→crate → gateway server.rs registration under #[cfg(feature)]
nostr-sdk v0.44 requires explicit feature flags for nip04 and nip44 — neither is in `default`
nostr-sdk prelude imports `nip44::{self, *}` causing name collision with nip04::decrypt; must use fully qualified `nostr_sdk::nips::nip04::decrypt` path
notifications() broadcast receiver must be created BEFORE client.connect() to avoid missing events; events are deduplicated and sent only once per event ID
Filter::pubkey() filters on #p tag (recipient), not the event author pubkey — correct for kind:4 DM subscriptions
ChannelsConfig uses #[serde(flatten)] for extra channel configs; adding named fields like `nostr` is fine but adding `matrix` as a named field was an unintended scope creep that needed reverting
The repo's biome formatter enforces method chain line-breaking: .split().map().filter() must each be on separate lines
The validate.rs channels section was restructured in main to use a channel_account with tools sub-key; merging required adapting nostr to this new schema shape
ChannelMessageMeta gained a sender_id field in main branch; bus.rs needed updating to populate it
OTP verification must check pending codes BEFORE the allowlist gate — otherwise approved-pending senders are blocked before they can submit their code (same pattern as Telegram's handlers.rs)

code.finding: String truncation at byte boundary causes panic on multibyte UTF-8; use str::floor_char_boundary() instead of direct byte slice
client.notifications() creates a temporary that is dropped immediately; must bind to a let binding before the loop
OtpState::new() takes a cooldown_secs argument, not zero-arg
Config must be Arc<RwLock<NostrAccountConfig>> shared between state and bus loop to avoid disconnected clone; allowlist parsed pubkeys cached here to avoid re-parsing on every DM
update_account_config must detect [REDACTED] sentinel in secret_key field and preserve existing key rather than overwriting with the placeholder
SeenTracker must implement is_empty() alongside len() to satisfy clippy::len_without_is_empty
Collapsible match: RelayPoolNotification::Message { message: RelayMessage::Event { event, .. }, .. } with guard condition can be a single match arm with let-chain

workflow: nostr-sdk Client::disconnect() returns () not Result — unlike most async client methods
nostr-sdk Client::subscribe() takes a single Filter, not Vec<Filter>
taplo fmt reformats TOML files alphabetically, touching unrelated crates (msteams, secret-store, webhooks) — cosmetic but noisy in diffs
Greptile review is triggered via gh api POST to dispatches and polled via check-runs; reviews appear as PR comments not check annotations
Cherry-pick onto origin/main is the correct strategy when branches have unrelated histories from squash merges; merge --allow-unrelated-histories creates 70+ conflicts
Integration test env vars use NOSTR_TEST_BOT_KEY / NOSTR_TEST_SENDER_KEY naming (not _SECRET_KEY suffix used in provider tests)
cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt --all includes JS files via biome integration — CI checks both Rust fmt and biome in the same step

friction: nostr-sdk v0.44 nip04/nip44 feature flags are undiscoverable — not in default, not prominently documented, required cargo metadata inspection
nostr_sdk::prelude::* glob import of nip44::{self, *} creates silent name collision with nip04::decrypt making lint messages confusing
The branch had unrelated git history from the worktree setup, requiring cherry-pick strategy instead of merge for main integration
notifications() receiver timing bug wasted multiple debug iterations — the root cause (receiver must be created before connect) is non-obvious from the API
OTP flow was structurally wired but functionally broken (verification gate ran after allowlist gate) — caught only in second Greptile pass
biome formatter enforces method chain breaking that rustfmt does not, causing a CI failure after the initial commit
taplo fmt silently reformats unrelated TOML files, adding noise to diffs
open_items: NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs — more complex event handling, less widely supported, deferred to future
NIP-65 relay list advertisement — advertise preferred relays via kind:10002
NIP-94 media attachments — file metadata support for inbound media
Metrics counters/histograms are wired but no Grafana dashboard or alerting thresholds defined
Outbound NIP-44 preference config option — currently always sends NIP-04 for compat, NIP-44 decryption works inbound only
Contract test suite (moltis_channels::contract) not run against NostrPlugin — requires valid credentials to start so skipped; needs a mock key approach
CI workflow for nostr integration tests (provider-integration.yml equivalent) — secrets added to GitHub but no workflow job created yet
Slack and Matrix are not listed on the website despite being supported channels — pre-existing gap noticed during Nostr website work
